op.name: polydiv
op.notes: Divide one polynomial by another. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.
op.summary: Returns the quotient and remainder of polynomial division.

blocks.text: Returns the quotient and remainder of polynomial division.

blocks.name: note
blocks.body:
This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.
blocks.raw:
.. note::
   This forms part of the old polynomial API. Since version 1.4, the
   new polynomial API defined in `numpy.polynomial` is preferred.
   A summary of the differences can be found in the
   :doc:`transition guide </reference/routines.polynomials>`.

blocks.text:
The input arrays are the coefficients (including any coefficients
equal to zero) of the "numerator" (dividend) and "denominator"
(divisor) polynomials, respectively.

desc_blocks.text: Coefficients, including those equal to zero, of the quotient.
Coefficients, including those equal to zero, of the remainder.

blocks.text:
Both `u` and `v` must be 0-d or 1-d (ndim = 0 or 1), but `u.ndim` need
not equal `v.ndim`. In other words, all four possible combinations -
``u.ndim = v.ndim = 0``, ``u.ndim = v.ndim = 1``,
``u.ndim = 1, v.ndim = 0``, and ``u.ndim = 0, v.ndim = 1`` - work.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> x = np.array([3.0, 5.0, 2.0])
>>> y = np.array([2.0, 1.0])
>>> np.polydiv(x, y)
(array([1.5 , 1.75]), array([0.25]))

blocks.text: poly, polyadd, polyder, polydiv, polyfit, polyint, polymul, polysub
